author: w3Lb
content: WHY: rg extracts pattern literals and memchrs whole files to reject non-matches before regex runs. ripsed line-iterates every candidate file even when 99% of a tree can't match. SCOPE: ripsed-core matcher + engine. Matcher gains a cheap prescreen(text: &str) -> bool: literal patterns use text.contains (memchr-backed via std); regex patterns extract a required literal where possible (regex-syntax can provide prefix/inner literals — or start simple: only prescreen when the pattern is literal or case-sensitive with an extractable prefix; fall back to 'always maybe' otherwise). engine::apply early-returns EngineOutput::unchanged when prescreen says no. Case-insensitive literal: lowercase-contains is wrong for Unicode — either skip prescreen for CI patterns or use the compiled regex's is_match on the whole buffer (still one pass instead of per-line). MEASURE: add a criterion bench with a 95%-non-matching corpus; record before/after in the issue. TESTS: prescreen never causes a false skip (proptest: prescreen(text)==false implies apply finds 0 changes), CI patterns unaffected, empty pattern edge case.
